evidence.ocr_text:
(i) Solve the inequality |2x − 7| < |2x − 9|.
(ii) Hence find the largest integer n satisfying the inequality |2 ln n − 7| < |2 ln n − 9|.
2 Find the exact value of ∫₁² (2e²ˣ − 1)² dx. Show all necessary working.
3 A curve has equation y = (3 + 2 ln x) / (1 + ln x). Find the exact gradient of the curve at the point for which y = 4. [5]

4 The polynomial p(x) is defined by
p(x) = ax³ + ax² − 15x − 18,
where a is a constant. It is given that (x − 2) is a factor of p(x).
(i) Find the value of a.
(ii) Using this value of a, factorise p(x) completely.
(iii) Hence solve the equation p(eʸ) = 0, giving the answer correct to 2 significant figures.

5 It is given that ∫₀ᵃ (3x² + 4 cos 2x − sin x) dx = 2, where a is a constant.
(i) Show that a = ∛(3 − 2 sin 2a − cos a).
(ii) Using the equation in part (i), show by calculation that 0.5 < a < 0.75.
(iii) Use an iterative formula, based on the equation in part (i), to find the value of a correct to 3 significant figures. Give the result of each iteration to 5 significant figures.

6 (a) Showing all necessary working, solve the equation
sec α cosec α = 7
for 0° < α < 90°.
(b) Showing all necessary working, solve the equation
sin(β + 20°) + sin(β − 20°) = 6 cos β
for 0° < β < 90°.

7 The equation of a curve is $x^2 - 4xy - 2y^2 = 1$.

(i) Find an expression for $\frac{dy}{dx}$ and show that the gradient of the curve at the point $(-1, 2)$ is $-\frac{5}{2}$. [5]
(ii) Show that the curve has no stationary points.
(iii) Find the x-coordinate of each of the points on the curve at which the tangent is parallel to the y-axis.

1 The polynomial f(x) is defined by
f(x) = x^4 - 3x^3 + 5x^2 - 6x + 11.
Find the quotient and remainder when f(x) is divided by (x^2 + 2).

(i) Solve the equation |4x + 5| = |x - 7|.
(ii) Hence, using logarithms, solve the equation |2^{y+2} + 5| = |2^y - 7|, giving the answer correct to 3 significant figures.

The variables x and y satisfy the equation y = kx^a, where k and a are constants. The graph of ln y against ln x is a straight line passing through the points (0.22, 3.96) and (1.32, 2.43), as shown in the diagram. Find the values of k and a correct to 3 significant figures.
diagram_elements: coordinate axes labeled 'ln y' (vertical) and 'ln x' (horizontal)

Text directly follows from Q2(ii) context carried from previous page
Diagram and text form a complete standalone part of Q2(ii)
Marks [5] indicate this is the full subpart

evidence.ocr_text:
4 The sequence x₁, x₂, x₃, … defined by
x₁ = 1,  x_{n+1} = x_n / ln(2x_n)
converges to the value α.
(i) Use the iterative formula to find the value of α correct to 4 significant figures. Give the result of each iteration to 6 significant figures.
(ii) State an equation satisfied by α and hence determine the exact value of α.
5 Find the exact coordinates of the stationary point of the curve with equation y = e^(-1/2 x)(2x + 5). [5]

(a) Show that ∫₂¹⁸ (3/(2x)) dx = ln 27.
(b) Find the exact value of ∫₀^(π/6) 4 sin²((3/2)x) dx. Show all necessary working.

7 The parametric equations of a curve are
x = 3 sin 2θ, y = 1 + 2 tan 2θ,
for 0 ≤ θ < ¼π.
(i) Find the exact gradient of the curve at the point for which θ = ⅙π.
(ii) Find the value of θ at the point where the gradient of the curve is 2, giving the value correct to 3 significant figures.

(i) Express 0.5 cos θ − 1.2 sin θ in the form R cos(θ + α), where R > 0 and 0° < α < 90°, giving the value of α correct to 2 decimal places.
(ii) Hence solve the equation 0.5 cos θ − 1.2 sin θ = 0.8 for 0° < θ < 360°.
(iii) Determine the greatest and least possible values of (3 − cos θ + 2.4 sin θ)² as θ varies.

(i) Solve the inequality |2x − 7| < |2x − 9|.
(ii) Hence find the largest integer n satisfying the inequality |2 ln n − 7| < |2 ln n − 9|.
2 Find the exact value of ∫₁² (2e²ˣ − 1)² dx. Show all necessary working.
3 A curve has equation y = (3 + 2 ln x) / (1 + ln x). Find the exact gradient of the curve at the point for which y = 4. [5]

4 The polynomial p(x) is defined by
p(x) = ax³ + ax² − 15x − 18,
where a is a constant. It is given that (x − 2) is a factor of p(x).
(i) Find the value of a.
(ii) Using this value of a, factorise p(x) completely.
(iii) Hence solve the equation p(eʸ) = 0, giving the answer correct to 2 significant figures.

5 It is given that ∫₀ᵃ (3x² + 4 cos 2x − sin x) dx = 2, where a is a constant.
(i) Show that a = ∛(3 − 2 sin 2a − cos a).
(ii) Using the equation in part (i), show by calculation that 0.5 < a < 0.75.
(iii) Use an iterative formula, based on the equation in part (i), to find the value of a correct to 3 significant figures. Give the result of each iteration to 5 significant figures.

6 (a) Showing all necessary working, solve the equation
sec α cosec α = 7
for 0° < α < 90°.
(b) Showing all necessary working, solve the equation
sin(β + 20°) + sin(β − 20°) = 6 cos β
for 0° < β < 90°.

7 The equation of a curve is x² - 4xy - 2y² = 1.
(i) Find an expression for dy/dx and show that the gradient of the curve at the point (-1, 2) is -5/2. [5]
(ii) Show that the curve has no stationary points.
(iii) Find the x-coordinate of each of the points on the curve at which the tangent is parallel to the y-axis.
